data.title: Kwan Yong Sees Sharp Rise in Fiscal 2025 Profit
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Kwan Yong (HKG:9998) reported SG$12.8 million in attributable profit for the fiscal year ended June 30, rising from SG$1.6 million a year earlier, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Earnings per share was SG$0.016 compared with SG$0.002 in the prior year.

Revenue rose 79% year-on-year to SG$239.9 million, figures showed.

The company also declared a dividend of HK$0.02 per share, payable Jan. 21, 2026, to shareholders of record on Dec. 30, 2025.

Shares of the investment firm gained over 20% in late morning trade Friday.

data.title: Frontier Services Group to Sell 3 Cessna Jets for $3 Million
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Frontier Services Group (HKG:0500) agreed to sell three Cessna Citation Bravo 550 light jets to Protea Group Management Services for $3 million, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the company fell over 2% in morning trade Friday.

The sale will help the company raise funds for the technical upgrade and maintenance of other aircraft.

data.title: Frasers and Neave to Dispose of 50% Stake in Malaysian Joint Venture
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Frasers and Neave (SGX:F99) decided to dispose of a 50% stake in joint venture company in Malaysia, Vacaron Company, to Tan and Tan Developments, according to a Friday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The move is part of the company's strategy to focus on its manufacturing, distribution, marketing and sale of its beverage, dairy and food products.

The joint venture with Frasers Property Malaysia owns a parcel of land measuring 5.149 hectares.

data.title: China Smarter Energy's H1 Loss Narrows
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China Smarter Energy Group (HKG:1004) reported HK$98.9 million in attributable loss for the first half of 2025, narrowing from a loss of HK$409.7 million a year earlier, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Loss per share was HK$0.0106 compared with HK$0.0437 in the prior year.

Revenue for the period rose 6.5% year-on-year to HK$38.2 million, figures showed.

Shares of the electric company remain suspended from trading.

data.title: Thailand budgets $1.46 bln for co-payment scheme to start in Oct, minister says
data.description:
Bangkok, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Thailand is preparing a budget of 47 billion baht ($1.46 billion) for a co-payment scheme that will start in October, Paradorn Prissananantakul, minister at the Prime Minister's office, said on Friday.

($1 = 32.21 baht)

Reporting by Chayut Setboonsarng, Panarat Thepgumpanat; Editing by David Stanway

data.title: Heineken Launches EUR2 Billion Three-tranche Notes Issue
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Dutch brewing company Heineken (HEIA.AS) issued three-tranche notes worth 2 billion euros, set to list on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange.

The first 500 million-euro tranche offers a coupon of 2.565% and will mature on Oct. 3, 2028. The other two tranches, worth 750 million euros each, carry coupons of 3.505% and 3.872%, respectively, with maturities on May 3, 2034, and Oct. 3, 2037, according to a Thursday release.

BNP Paribas, Deutsche Bank, ING, JP Morgan, and Rabobank were the active bookrunners for the issue.

data.title: Breton Technology Wins Potential Contract For Electric Mining Trucks; Shares Jump 19%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Breton Technology (HKG:1333) won a potential contract for its autonomous electric mining trucks from Xinjiang Mingyang Mining Construction Group, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the electric-powered engineering machinery company gained nearly 19% in late-morning trade Friday.

The firm entered into a so-called strategic cooperation agreement where both sides agreed to cooperate on electrified and autonomous mining transportation.

As part of that agreement, Xinjiang Mingyang Mining intends to acquire at least 1,000 electric mining trucks from the company by Sept. 22, 2028.

data.title: India central bank likely steps into NDF market to support rupee after US tariff jolt
data.description:
Mumbai, Sept 26 (Reuters) - The Reserve Bank of India likely stepped into the non-deliverable forwards (NDF) market before the local market opened on Friday to support the rupee, five traders told Reuters, as the currency came under pressure after the US announced steep tariffs on imported branded drugs.

The Indian rupee was on course to open near its all-time low of 88.7975 against the US dollar but recovered following the intervention. It opened at 88.6850 at 9:00 a.m. IST (0330 GMT), nearly flat from its close in the pervious session.

The rupee has fallen about 0.5% in September amid worries over the impact of steep US tariffs on Indian exports and higher H1-B visa fees.

(Reporting by Jaspreet Kalra and Dharamraj Dhutia; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

data.title: CSC Unit to Acquire 51% Stake in AccX via Share Subscription; CSC Shares Rise 17%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- CSC Holdings (HKG:0235, SGX:C06) said its wholly owned unit agreed to subscribe for 255,000 convertible preference shares in AccX Limited for about $4.37 million, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Hong Kong-listed shares of the engineering services provider surged over 17% in Friday morning trade.

The investment will give CSC an indirect 51% stake in AccX, which will become a non-wholly owned subsidiary of the group upon completion.

Proceeds from the investment will be used to repay start-up loans, fund working capital and R&D, and support expansion, CSC said.

The company added that the acquisition aligns with its strategy to diversify into Southeast Asia's financial services market and may create synergies with its earlier purchase of Citystate Savings Bank in the Philippines.

AccX and its affiliates provide digital payment services in Southeast Asia, the filing said.

data.title: Volkswagen to Temporarily Halt Production at Two German EV Plants
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Volkswagen (VOW.F) plans to temporarily halt production at two German factories that exclusively produce electric vehicles amid slower-than-expected demand for EVs.

Citing a spokesperson for the automaker's Zwickau site, Bloomberg News reported Thursday that production will be halted for one week starting Oct. 6. Meanwhile, the Emden plant has trimmed employee hours and is expected to shut down production for several days, the report added, citing people familiar with the situation.

Volkswagen did not immediately respond to MT Newswires' request for comment.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Materials Sector Rises, Healthcare Falls
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Materials stocks advanced almost 1% at midday Friday.

Stronger-than-expected second-quarter US GDP data decreased expectations for rate cuts in the US. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq indices fell 0.5% and the Dow Jones index declined almost 0.4%.

Fortescue (ASX:FMG) finalized the acquisition of Spanish renewable technology firm Nabrawind, consolidating its previous minority stake to take full ownership. Under the firm's agreement with XCMG, it is expected to supply up to half of Fortescue's future fleet of 300 to 400 zero-emissions 240-tonne haul trucks, with phased deliveries planned from 2028 to 2030.

Liebherr, with manufacturing facilities in the US, is expected to provide at a minimum the other half of the haul truck fleet and electric excavators under a partnership with Fortescue.

On the flip side, the healthcare sector fell over 1% after sector heavyweight CSL (ASX:CSL) declined over 2% in recent trading.

The decline came after US President Donald Trump warned of a 100% tariff on imports of branded or patented pharmaceutical products, effective Oct.1, unless the company is constructing a manufacturing plant in the US.

data.title: Seazen Terminates HK$50 Million Share Placement
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Seazen Group (HKG:1030) terminated a share placement to Blockchain Metaverse Academy that would have raised around HK$50 million, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Shares of the property developer gained nearly 2% in morning trade Friday.

The company said it was no longer selling the shares due to a number of factors, including current market conditions.

data.title: Lawsuits Against Matex International Discontinued
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Matex International (SGX:M15) said two lawsuits against the company had been discontinued following a court hearing and judgment.

The company no longer has any outstanding lawsuits.

Meanwhile, the trading suspension on the company's shares remains in effect.

data.title: Thailand's credit rating not a concern, no VAT hike plan, official says
data.description:
Bangkok, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Thailand's credit rating is not a concern due to very low levels of foreign debt, a deputy finance minister said on Friday, adding that the government is now trying to boost the economy.

There is no proposal to raise value added tax at the moment, deputy minister Vorapak Tanyawong also told reporters.

(Reporting by Kitiphong Thaichareon; Writing by Orathai Sriring; Editing by David Stanway)

data.title: Eli Lilly Obtains US FDA Approval for Breast Cancer Medication Inluriyo
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Eli Lilly (LLY.SW) said Thursday the US Food and Drug Administration approved Inluriyo, or imlunestrant, as a treatment for adults with estrogen receptor-positive, human epidermal growth factor receptor 2-negative and ESR1-mutated advanced or metastatic breast cancer.

Specifically, the treatment was approved for patients whose disease progressed after at least one line of endocrine therapy. Availability in the US is expected in the coming weeks.

The pharmaceutical company supported its application for Inluriyo with findings from the phase 3 Ember-3 trial. Enrolment is ongoing for the phase 3 Ember-4 trial.

data.title: Shoucheng's Beijing Robot Fund Invests in Motorevo Robotics
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Shoucheng (HKG:0697) said its Beijing Robot Industry Development Investment Fund has invested in Motorevo Robotics, a domestic developer and manufacturer of integrated rotary joints, according to a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Shares of the infrastructure asset management firm rose over 1% in Friday morning trade.

The funding will support Motorevo's research and development, production upgrades, and expansion into broader application scenarios.

The investment will also be used to build talent pipelines, improve manufacturing capacity, and strengthen quality management systems.

Motorevo's product portfolio covers humanoid robots, robotic dogs, robotic arms, and exoskeletons, according to the filing.

data.title: India New Issue-Bajaj Finance to issue 5-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
MUMBAI, Sept 26 (Reuters) - India's Bajaj Finance plans to raise 20 billion
rupees ($225.3 million) through the sale of bonds maturing in five years, three bankers said on
Friday.
    It has invited coupon and commitment bids for the issue later in the day, they said.
    The company did not immediately respond to a Reuters email seeking comment.
    
Here is the list of deals reported so far on September 26:
                                                                      
   Issuer   Tenure        Coupon (in %)  Issue size (in  Bidding      
                                         bln rupees)*    date         Rating
                                                                      
                                                                      
 Bajaj           5 years  To be decided            5+15  September    AAA
 Finance                                                 26           (Crisil
                                                                      )
 
*Size includes base plus greenshoe for some issues


($1 = 88.7560 Indian rupees)

 (Reporting by Khushi Malhotra and Dharamraj Dhutia; Editing by Sumana Nandy)

data.title: EssilorLuxottica's Essilor Stellest Lens Secures US FDA Market Authorization
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- EssilorLuxottica (EL.PA) received market authorization from the US Food and Drug Administration for its Essilor Stellest lens, according to a Thursday release.

The European eyewear company's lens has been clinically proven to slow down myopia progression in children by 71% on average over two years. The vision care product already obtained the US FDA's Breakthrough Device designation in 2021.

EssilorLuxottica noted that the product will be available in the US in the coming weeks.

data.title: South Korea to Offer Around-the-Clock Forex Trading
data.description:
By Kwanwoo Jun


South Korea plans to operate its currency market around the clock, giving foreign financial institutions and investors better access to its money market.

The Finance Ministry said Friday that it will announce a road map within this year to detail the plan to allow won trading on a 24-hour basis. It will also outline other measures to improve financial systems, aimed at getting the country upgraded to a developed-market status by MSCI.

Limited foreign-exchange trading in South Korea has long been a stumbling block to the country's push for a market upgrade by MSCI.

The ministry said in a statement that the road map will also outline a plan to establish an offshore settlement system to address inconveniences faced by foreigners trading won.

The ministry didn't specify a timeline for implementing the new measures, which Seoul officials say could take effect sometime next year.

In 2024, South Korea extended its forex trading hours in a continued push to advance its market.

The country's forex market currently operates from 9 a.m. through 2 a.m. local time on weekdays, more than doubling its daily trading hours previously.


Write to Kwanwoo Jun at kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

09-25-25 2317ET

data.title: Concord Healthcare Secures Financing From Beijing Daxing Leasing; Shares Slip 2%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Concord Healthcare (HKG:2453) has entered into a financing agreement with Beijing Daxing Development Finance Leasing, which will provide up to 20 million yuan over three years at an interest rate of 4.19%, a Thursday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

Shares of the firm were down nearly 2% in Friday morning trade.

The leasing unit is affiliated with Beijing Daxing Development State-owned Capital Investment & Operation, a Chinese state-owned enterprise, the filing said.

Concord said the arrangement will broaden its funding channels and support future business development, while also strengthening ties with industry institutions.

data.title: HSBC Says Quantum Trading Pilot With IBM Improved Bond Pricing Accuracy
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- HSBC (HKG:0005) said it has run the "world's first-known" live test of quantum-enabled algorithmic trading, working with IBM to apply quantum computing to corporate bond pricing, according to a Thursday press release.

The trial showed up to a 34% improvement in predicting the likelihood of winning customer inquiries in Europe's corporate bond market compared with standard classical techniques, the lender said.

HSBC said the trial, combined with multiple IBM quantum computers, revealed hidden pricing signals in noisy market data, boosting the accuracy of trade-fill predictions in over-the-counter bond markets.

"It means we now have a tangible example of how today's quantum computers could solve a real-world business problem at scale," said Philip Intallura, HSBC's group head of quantum technologies.

Jay Gambetta, vice president of IBM Quantum, said the results highlight the potential of combining classical approaches with quantum's computational space as the technology matures.

data.title: New Concepts to Sell HK$31 Million Worth of Shares to Five Investors
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- New Concepts (HKG:2221) said it agreed to sell around HK$31 million worth of shares to five investors, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Thursday.

Shares of the environmental protection company gained nearly 37% in morning trade Friday.

The company plans to sell 20,570,000 shares priced at HK$1.50 each to raise funds for repaying amounts owed by the firm.

The shares represent around 11.26% of the company's issued share capital, and 10.12% on an enlarged basis.

data.title: BYD Makes Senior Hires to Revive Momentum in Germany
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- BYD (HKG:1211, SHE:002594) is stepping up efforts in Germany with a string of senior appointments following a recent slowdown in sales, Bloomberg News reported Thursday.

The Chinese EV maker named four new managers, including Christian Lochner, formerly with Fiat Chrysler and Mitsubishi Motors (TYO:7211), as key account manager for fleet sales and dealer support, according to the report.

Birk Pfennig, who previously worked at Hyundai Motor (KRX:005380) and Volkswagen's Seat brand, was reportedly appointed to lead retail sales.

The company has also tapped several former Stellantis executives in recent months, including Maria Grazia Davino, ex-head of its UK business, and Alessandro Grosso, former vice president of Italy sales, it added.

The hires highlight BYD's bid to regain momentum in a market dominated by Volkswagen, Mercedes-Benz, and BMW, while also facing new pressure from Stellantis' Chinese partner Leapmotor, Bloomberg wrote.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Baidu's Robotaxi Unit Eyes New Markets
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Baidu's (HKG:9888) robotaxi unit Apollo Go is in talks with authorities in Australia and parts of Southeast Asia to introduce autonomous vehicles to the countries, Bloomberg reported Thursday.

The firm recently received authorization to double its fleet to around 100 cars in the United Arab Emirates, and is in talks to bring autonomous vehicles to newer markets, the report said, citing an interview with Halton Niu, general manager for the division's overseas business.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Japan has most-favoured nation status for US pharmaceutical tariffs, Akazawa says
data.description:
Tokyo, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Japan has secured the United States' most-favoured nation status on its tariffs on chips and drugs, Tokyo's trade negotiator Ryosei Akazawa said on Friday, after U.S. President Donald Trump announced new sector-specific tariff measures.

Akazawa, while refraining from comments on Trump's new duties on branded drugs and heavy-duty trucks, said an existing U.S.-Japan joint statement has confirmed U.S. tariff rates on Japanese semiconductors and pharmaceuticals would not exceed those to others like the European Union.

"We will continue to analyse U.S. tariff measures and their impact on Japan once they become clear, including how they relate to the current U.S.-Japan agreement," Akazawa told a regular press conference.
(Reporting by Kantaro Komiya; Editing by Christian Schmollinger)

data.title: China's Net Gold Imports via Hong Kong Slide 39% in August From July
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China's net gold imports via Hong Kong dropped 39.1% to 26.7 metric tons in August from 43.9 tons in July, Reuters reported Thursday, citing data from Hong Kong's Census and Statistics Department.

The figures offer only a partial view, as China also channels imports through Shanghai and Beijing, the news agency said.

Total imports via Hong Kong slid 29.9% month over month to 40.9 tons, according to the report.

The decline coincided with a 3.4% drop in China's gold imports in August from July, according to customs data, the report added.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Beijing HCRT Electrical Equipments Opens 176% Higher in Shenzhen Trading Debut
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Beijing HCRT Electrical Equipments (SHE:301668) surged 176% to 58 yuan during its first trading day on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange Friday.

The power distribution equipment company priced its IPO at 21 yuan per share.

data.title: China's Lottery Sales Down 2% in August
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- China saw lottery ticket sales of 53.4 billion yuan in August, down 1.8% from the previous year, according to data from the Ministry of Finance.

This brought lottery ticket sales for the first eight months of the year to 423.2 billion yuan, up 1.4% year over year.

data.title: Shares of CSL Fall After Trump Warns of 100% Tariffs on Pharmaceutical Products Imports
data.description: (MT Newswires) -- Shares of CSL (ASX:CSL) fell past 2% in recent Friday trade after US President Donald Trump warned of a 100% tariff on imports of branded or patented pharmaceutical products, effective Oct.1, unless the company has started construction of a manufacturing plant in the US.

data.title: Xiaomi Weighs Europe EV Plant as Overseas Expansion Gains Pace
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Xiaomi (HKG:1810) is considering building electric vehicle factories in Europe as part of its overseas growth strategy, The Standard reported Thursday, citing Vice President and Chief Marketing Officer Xu Fei at a press conference.

According to the report, the company conducted site inspections in the region in June and may first open automobile showrooms.

Xiaomi has previously flagged plans to enter the European market in 2027. While Xu did not specify which model would debut, it is expected that the firm will not develop an entirely new vehicle for the region, The Standard said.

Separately, Xiaomi aims to operate 10,000 self-run Xiaomi Home stores worldwide by 2030.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Seres Plans Hong Kong Share Listing
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Seres (SHA:601127) plans to offer up to 331,477,235 shares on the Hong Kong stock exchange, according to a Friday disclosure to the Shanghai bourse.

The planned share listing is subject to approval by securities regulators in Hong Kong and mainland China, the filing said.

data.title: Shanghai copper inches up on Freeports panic; high prices limit gain
data.description:
SHANGHAI, Sept 26 (Reuters) - Shanghai copper extended gains on Friday with the market supported by supply disruption concerns following Freeport's force majeure declaration but gains were limited by weaker demand amid high prices.

The most-traded copper contract on Shanghai Futures Exchange gained 0.48% to trade at 82,470 yuan ($11,559) per metric ton as of 0212 GMT.

The benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange was up 0.03% to $10,263 a ton as of 0212 GMT.

Shanghai copper price climbed to a half-year high on Thursday after Freeport said it expected its Indonesian unit's output would be 35% lower in 2026 than previously estimated.

The high prices come amid restocking ahead of China's National Day holiday from October 1 to October 8, which is expected to impact demand, with further gains in copper price depending on the downstream acceptance of high copper prices, analysts at Chinese broker Haitong Futures said.

Meanwhile, China is studying measures to regulate its copper smelting capacity, a state news outlet reported on Thursday, citing Chen Xuesen, vice chairman at the China Nonferrous Metals Industry Association.

Copper enterprises should firmly oppose "involution-style" competition, which hurts the interest for both the industry and country, Chen said.

Among other LME metals, nickel nudged down 0.19%, lead was down 0.42%, while aluminium, zinc and tin stayed little changed.

For SHFE base metals, nickel tumbled 0.87%, zinc added 0.3%, lead dipped 0.18%, while aluminium and tin were little changed.

($1 = 7.1345 Chinese yuan renminbi) (Reporting by Dylan Duan and Lewis Jackson; Editing by Mrigank Dhaniwala)

data.title: Horizon Robotics Seeks HK$6.4 Billion via Share Offering
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Horizon Robotics (HKG:9660) is looking to raise HK$6.38 billion via a share offering, according to a Hong Kong bourse filing Friday.

A number of the company's shareholders will sell 639,028,800 shares at HK$9.99 apiece to the public. Then, the shareholders will purchase an equal number of shares that will be issued by the company at the same price.

The shares to be sold represent 4.6% of the company's existing issued share capital and 1.9% of its voting rights, and 4.4% of its share capital and 1.9% of its voting rights on an enlarged basis.

Horizon Robotics said it would use proceeds from the offering to expand its business, particularly overseas.

Shares of the robotics company fell over 5% in pre-market trade.

data.title: Jacobio Pharmaceuticals' Concert Parties Lift Stake to Over 25%
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Jacobio Pharmaceuticals (HKG:1167) said parties acting in concert have purchased about 11.1 million shares since mid-July for HK$96.3 million, according to a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing.

Their combined stake now stands at 200.2 million shares, or roughly 25.3% of the company.

Additionally, the pharmaceutical firm repurchased 326,400 shares for HK$2.7 million under its ongoing HK$100 million buyback program, the filing said.

data.title: Japan to launch facility to support $550 billion investment under US trade deal
data.description:
TOKYO (Reuters) -Japan's finance ministry said on Friday that it will set up an investment facility at a state-owned development bank to support a $550 billion investment package agreed in Tokyo's tariff deal with Washington.

Japan and the United States signed a memorandum of understanding on the details of the package this month, stating it would focus on investments in sectors such as chips, metals, pharmaceuticals, energy and shipbuilding to be made by January 2029, which coincides with the end of Donald Trump's presidential term.

Japan's investment package would include equity, loans and loan guarantees from the state-owned Japan Bank for International Cooperation (JBIC) and Nippon Export and Investment Insurance (NEXI).

The new facility at JBIC will provide financial support for overseas expansions by Japanese companies in industries strategically important for Japan's economic security, the ministry said.

The ministry also said it revised regulations on JBIC to expand the scope of its investment in developed countries, including the automotive and pharmaceutical industries.

The previous regulations limit JBIC's scope of investments in developed countries to certain sectors compared to those in emerging countries.

(Reporting by Makiko Yamazaki; Editing by Saad Sayeed)

By Makiko Yamazaki

data.title: Dynamic Electronics to Offer Up to 52.5 Million Shares in Shanghai IPO
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Dynamic Electronics (SHA:603175) plans to offer up to 52.5 million shares in an initial public offering on the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

The printed circuit board manufacturer's shares on offer will represent 12.01% of its entire share capital following the IPO, according to a Friday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

Upon completion, Dynamic Electronics' total share capital will rise to 437,029,321 shares.

Guolian Minsheng Securities Underwriting and Sponsoring is the sponsor and lead underwriter of the offering.

data.title: Shanghai Refire to Raise HK$78 Million via Share Placement
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Shanghai Refire (HKG:2570) expects to raise about HK$78.3 million in net proceeds through the placement of 583,860 new H shares at HK$137.00 each, according to a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing.

The placing price represents a 14.2% discount to the last close and a 19.2% discount to the five-day average.

The new shares represent about 1.4% of Refire's existing H shares and are equivalent to about 0.7% of its total issued capital.

Net proceeds will be used to repay bank loans and lease liabilities, and for general corporate purposes.

data.title: Japanese Stocks Drop Amid Strong US Economic Data, Steady Tokyo Inflation
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Japanese equities opened lower on Friday, mirroring a lackluster trading on Wall Street spurred by strong US economic data and mixed Federal Reserve signals.

The Nikkei 225 opened 120.73 points or 0.26% lower at 45,634.20.

Strong recent US GDP figures have complicated the outlook for further monetary easing, shifting market focus to Friday's pivotal inflation report.

At home, Tokyo's core inflation rate held steady at 2.5% in September, reinforcing expectations that the Bank of Japan will remain cautious about further rate hikes despite hints of a potential move as early as October.

Elsewhere, market sentiment is also being weighed down by new US tariff threats, a pivotal earnings season, and the looming risk of a US government shutdown.

data.title: Tokyo Core Inflation Steady at 2.5% in September
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- Tokyo's core inflation, which excludes fresh food items, stood at 2.5% in September, unchanged from a month earlier, data released by Statistics of Japan on Friday showed.

Excluding fresh food items and energy, the reading fell to 2.5% in September compared with 3.0% the previous month.

The headline CPI, which includes all items, rose 2.5% from a year earlier, the same reading as in August, following the government's temporary subsidies.

Food prices, excluding perishables, grew by 6.9% in August, at a slower pace relative to the 7.4% rise a month before.

The data increases bets on the Bank of Japan's cautious approach to further interest rate hikes. A central bank member has recently hinted that a rate hike could happen as early as October.

data.title: Chiba Bank Confirms Talks With Chiba Kogyo On Possible Integration
data.description:
(MT Newswires) -- The Chiba Bank (TYO:8331) is in talks with Chiba Kogyo Bank (TYO:8337) about a potential business integration, but no decisions have been made, according to a Friday filing on the Tokyo Stock Exchange.

The bank said media reports on a holding company plan were not based on its disclosures and pledged to announce any decisions once finalized.
